{{Infobox concept
| name = Underlord
| introduced = cradle-02-soulsmith
}}

''See also: [[Underlord Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|An Underlord is a high-ranking [[sacred artist]] who has advanced beyond the [[Truegold]] stage. This progression requires clearing specific developmental milestones, such as opening a space within one's soul or weaving [[soulfire]] from [[aura]]. Reaching this rank is considered an extraordinary feat that defies common sense, as it represents a massive leap in power. Underlords possess enhanced physical capabilities, such as the strength associated with an [[Iron body]], and significantly expanded sensory perception. They also require less sleep than lower-ranked artists, though they still need rest to maintain peak condition. In the social hierarchy of the [[Blackflame Empire]] and various other factions, Underlords hold immense prestige and are often treated with the respect afforded to royalty. They can serve as powerful leaders for clans or emissaries for larger organizations, and their presence can dictate the political standing of an entire family. While they are formidable enough to rule distant lands or command large alliances, they remain a tier below the even more powerful [[Overlords]] and Archlords. Despite their strength, Underlords can still be vulnerable to specialized techniques or the overwhelming power of entities like [[Dreadgods]].}}

== Soulsmith ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|The Underlord is a [[sacred artist]] rank that follows the [[Truegold]] stage of advancement. While this title represents a distant dream for ordinary [[Iron]] practitioners, it is the level that allowed the [[Jai clan]] to rise to prominence. [[Eithan]] holds this specific rank, which marks a realm beyond [[Truegold]]. Currently, the Underlord is an entity that intends to arrive at the [[Ruins]].}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Underlord]] is a stage of advancement.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c7">''Soulsmith'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|The production of an [[Underlord]] allowed the [[Jai clan]] to rise to prominence.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c7" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Underlord]] is a title or rank.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c9">''Soulsmith'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Underlord]] is a rank above [[Truegold]].<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|An [[Underlord]] is a [[sacred artist]] who has reached the realm beyond [[Truegold]].<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c11">''Soulsmith'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|The [[Underlord]] is an entity that intends to arrive at the [[Ruins]].<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c15">''Soulsmith'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Underlord]] is a stage that is considered a distant dream for ordinary [[Iron]] practitioners.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c16">''Soulsmith'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Underlord]] is a rank or title held by [[Eithan]].<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c19">''Soulsmith'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|[[Underlord]] is an advancement stage that follows [[Truegold]].<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c20">''Soulsmith'', chapter 20</ref>}}

== Blackflame ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|The Underlord is a powerful rank of individual capable of claiming that [[Fisher Gesha]] neglected [[Lindon]]'s education. This status is held by [[Eithan]], who was addressed as such by [[Yerin]] and was responsible for the death of [[Kral]]. As an Underlord, [[Eithan]] possesses the ability to weave [[soulfire]] and hoard it for use during emergencies. During his time in the story, he raised a white spear into the sky.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|The [[Underlord]] is a person who could claim [[Fisher Gesha]] was neglecting [[Lindon]]'s education.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c1">''Blackflame'', chapter 1</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Fisher Gesha]] told [[Lindon]] not to tell the [[Underlord]] she helped him.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c1" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|The [[Underlord]] is responsible for the death of [[Kral]].<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c2">''Blackflame'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|An [[Underlord]] is a powerful rank/status.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c3">''Blackflame'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Yerin]] addresses [[Eithan]] as [[Underlord]].<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c5">''Blackflame'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Eithan]] is an [[Underlord]].<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c8">''Blackflame'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|Underlords weave [[soulfire]] and hoard it against emergencies.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c10">''Blackflame'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|The [[Underlord]] raised a white spear into the sky.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c16">''Blackflame'', chapter 16</ref>}}

== Skysworn ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The Underlords are waiting in the room.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c3">''Skysworn'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|An [[Underlord]] can destroy someone with a casual backhand.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c9">''Skysworn'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|[[Underlord]] is a rank [[Lindon]] could potentially reach in his fifties.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c12">''Skysworn'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|The [[Underlord]] is a person to whom [[Lindon]] and [[Renfei]] must bring [[Lindon]].<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c13">''Skysworn'', chapter 13</ref>}}

== Ghostwater ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|Underlords and above have bodies reforged by [[soulfire]].<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c9">''Ghostwater'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-05-ghostwater|It would take an [[Underlord]] at least to search for someone from many miles away using spiritual sense.<ref name="cradle-05-ghostwater-c11">''Ghostwater'', chapter 11</ref>}}

== Underlord ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|The Underlord represents a specific rank of power that involves remaking an individual's body and spirit through transformative resonance. To reach this stage, a practitioner must master themselves, sense the unity of [[aura]] to master their surroundings, and discover their reason for practicing the [[sacred arts]]. While [[Lindon]] has not yet reached this stage himself, he noted that [[Yerin]] possessed the potential to become an Underlord if given his opportunities. During a conflict, thirteen of the sixteen known Underlords gathered on a cloudship deck, with several dashing through trees to launch [[Striker techniques]] at [[the Emperor]]'s ship. Although Underlords are formidable, Truegolds possess enough strength to stand up to them.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|[[Underlord]] is a path or stage [[Yerin]] is focusing on.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c3">''Underlord'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|[[Lindon]] suggested [[Yerin]] would be an [[Underlord]] if she had his chances.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|Reaching [[Underlord]] involves three steps: mastering yourself, mastering the world around you, and connecting to the world.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c5">''Underlord'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|The second step to reaching [[Underlord]] is sensing the unity of [[aura]].<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c5" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|The third step to reaching [[Underlord]] is discovering one's reason for practicing the [[sacred arts]].<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c5" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|An [[Underlord]] is a stage achieved by burning away one's old body and spirit through transformative resonance.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c9">''Underlord'', chapter 9</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|The opponent was a lone [[Underlord]].<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c9" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|[[Kiro]] has an [[Underlord body]].<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c10">''Underlord'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|Advancing to [[Underlord]] remakes the body and spirit.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c12">''Underlord'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|[[Underlord]] is a stage or rank [[Lindon]] has not yet reached.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c14">''Underlord'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|Underlords are a rank of power.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c15">''Underlord'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|Thirteen of the sixteen Underlords were gathered on the cloudship deck.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c17">''Underlord'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|Underlords were dashing through trees and pitching [[Striker techniques]] at [[the Emperor]]'s ship.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c19">''Underlord'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|Truegolds can stand up to Underlords.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c19" />}}

== Uncrowned ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|Achieving the rank of Underlord grants an individual the ability to cast their senses much wider and farther. During the selection process for the main [[Akura]] team, sixteen young Underlords were identified as candidates. Among those present was a gold [[dragon]] possessing the power of an Underlord, while a man in the second trial revealed his own spirit to be of that rank. As the competition progressed, fifteen young Underlords remained in the center of the [[arena]], and two Underlords were seen bowing.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|Reaching [[Underlord]] allows senses to be cast wider and farther.<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c4">''Uncrowned'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|Sixteen young Underlords are candidates for the main [[Akura]] team.<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c7">''Uncrowned'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|The gold [[dragon]] has the power of an [[Underlord]].<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c8">''Uncrowned'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|The man in the second trial revealed his spirit was an [[Underlord]].<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c11">''Uncrowned'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|There were two Underlords who bowed.<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c16">''Uncrowned'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|Fifteen young Underlords are in the center of the [[arena]].<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c21">''Uncrowned'', chapter 21</ref>}}

== Wintersteel ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The Underlord stage represents a specific revelation achieved by [[Lindon]] through the process of seeing oneself clearly. To advance to this level, an individual must condense their [[soulfire]] and fill themselves with it, pushing their [[madra]] to the absolute peak their soul can contain. These powerful beings, such as the [[Ninecloud]] peacekeepers, often hover upon Thousand-Mile Clouds and possess enough strength to walk directly up to a [[Dreadgod]]. While an Underlord is a rank that [[Ziel]] might accept if he fails to regain [[Archlord]] status, the level is significant enough that even [[Heralds]] or Archlords might refrain from interfering if an Underlord has someone in their grip.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Ninecloud]] peacekeepers are Underlords.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c3">''Wintersteel'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|Underlords hover on Thousand-Mile Clouds.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c7">''Wintersteel'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|Underlords advance by condensing their [[soulfire]], filling themselves with it, and pushing their [[madra]] to the peak of power their souls can contain.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c7" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Underlord]] is a stage or revelation [[Lindon]] has achieved.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c8">''Wintersteel'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The two students watching the [[Sage]] are both [[Underlord]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c8" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|An [[Underlord]] enters the room carrying four boxes.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c8" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|The [[Underlord revelation]] was based on seeing oneself clearly.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c14">''Wintersteel'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|An [[Underlord]] is a level of power [[Ziel]] might settle for if he cannot regain [[Archlord]] status.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c15">''Wintersteel'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|Underlords are capable of walking right up to the [[Dreadgod]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c23">''Wintersteel'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|An [[Underlord]] is a rank that Archlords and [[Heralds]] might not interfere with if they are in their grip.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c25">''Wintersteel'', chapter 25</ref>}}

== Bloodline ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Underlord]] is a level of power or status.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c2">''Bloodline'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Normally an [[Underlord]] cannot do anything to a [[Dreadgod]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c19">''Bloodline'', chapter 19</ref>}}

== Reaper ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|Advancement to [[Underlord]] is expected to fix [[Jai Long]]'s facial condition.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c6">''Reaper'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Eithan]] refers to himself as an [[Underlord]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c21">''Reaper'', chapter 21</ref>}}

== Dreadgod ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|Underlords are powerful entities that can be engaged in combat when dealing with a [[Remnant]]. During these events, [[Orthos]] demonstrates strength equivalent to an Underlord before officially ascending to the rank himself. As the conflict concludes, an Underlord launches an attack. Such entities can be successfully defeated through the use of a [[Striker technique]].}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|Underlords are entities that can be fought when dealing with a [[Remnant]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c7">''Dreadgod'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Orthos]] possesses [[Underlord]] strength.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c10">''Dreadgod'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Orthos]] is now an [[Underlord]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c15">''Dreadgod'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|An [[Underlord]] attacked at the end of the chapter.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c15" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|An [[Underlord]] can be defeated by a [[Striker technique]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c19">''Dreadgod'', chapter 19</ref>}}

== Waybound ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|[[Malice]] encouraged [[Mercy]] to reach [[Underlord]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c28">''Waybound'', chapter 28</ref>}}

== Threshold: Stories from Cradle ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Underlord]] is an advancement level.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c1">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 1</ref>}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|“The Underlord…killed him?” his little sister asked.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c2" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|“We have to bring him to the Underlord either way,” Renfei said at last.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c13" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|“If you got the same chances I did, you’d be Underlord by now.”<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c3" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|“There are three steps to reaching Underlord. Mastering yourself, mastering the world around you, and then connecting yourself to the world.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c5" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

[[Category:Concepts]]
[[Category:Soulsmith]]
[[Category:Blackflame]]
[[Category:Skysworn]]
[[Category:Ghostwater]]
[[Category:Underlord]]
[[Category:Uncrowned]]
[[Category:Wintersteel]]
[[Category:Bloodline]]
[[Category:Reaper]]
[[Category:Dreadgod]]
[[Category:Waybound]]
[[Category:Threshold: Stories from Cradle]]
[[Category:Cradle]]
[[Category:Cradle concepts]]
